metadata.title: Core Korean: Places and Food
metadata.source_description: Supplemental A1 vocab selected from coverage gaps

examples.english: I buy water at a store.

item.notes: Coverage gap: places. Basic general place word useful before more specific shop names.
item.image_prompt: Polished digital illustration of a small neighborhood store in Korea with shelves and a customer at the entrance, natural street setting, no text.

image.prompt: Polished digital illustration of a small neighborhood store in Korea with shelves and a customer at the entrance, natural street setting, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.

cards.back_html:
store
가게
gage

Examples

  • 가게에서 물을 사요.
    I buy water at a store.
Coverage gap: places. Basic general place word useful before more specific shop names.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • places

가게
store
gage

Examples

  • 가게에서 물을 사요.
    I buy water at a store.
Coverage gap: places. Basic general place word useful before more specific shop names.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • places

item.image_prompt: Polished digital illustration of a Korean family sitting together in a modern living room, warm natural interaction, no text.

image.prompt: Polished digital illustration of a Korean family sitting together in a modern living room, warm natural interaction, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.

가족
gajok

Examples

  • 가족이 있어요.
    I have a family.
Coverage gap: family. Foundational family word used in introductions and many beginner contexts.
